target: Caption link UX fix (expand eg-evaluate on click) and thesis-hero structure test

patches.description: ISS-1612/1642/1711: The 'Read the long form →' caption in ds-evaluate-preface calls showTab('evaluate') which switches to the evaluate tab, but <details id='eg-evaluate'> is collapsed by default. The reader sees the section collapsed and must click the summary to expand. Adding d.open=true expands the details element immediately so the full 'How to Evaluate Any Scientific Claim' prose is visible. Applies to generate-html.js line 1541 (the ds-ep-jump link in the Five Questions caption). Note: the fm-jump link at line 1513 href='#eg-falsifiability' is a different target and does not need this fix.

patches.find_pattern: assert(thesisPos < barPos, 'EXP-271: ds-thesis-hero must appear BEFORE ds-verdict-bars (claim → result)');
patches.description: ISS-1648: test.js only checked positional ordering of ds-thesis-hero (thesisPos > -1). Adding 4 structural assertions: ds-thesis-statement (the headline claim paragraph), ds-thesis-badge (the live-test pulse indicator), ds-thesis-figures (the 4-column stat grid), and id='ds-thesis-statement' (the aria-labelledby target). These assert that the thesis-hero component renders fully, not just that its wrapper element exists. Applied after the EXP-271 ordering assertion at line 746.
